title: The History of Paper
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Paper is (0) such a common material today that we rarely think about its fascinating history. However, its invention was a revolutionary development that transformed communication and the spread of knowledge.||The credit for inventing paper goes (9) to Cai Lun, a Chinese court official who developed the process around 105 CE. Before paper, the Chinese wrote (10) on bamboo strips and silk, which were either too heavy or too expensive for widespread use. Cai Lun's paper was made from tree bark, hemp, and old fishing nets, creating (11) a lightweight and affordable writing material.||The knowledge of papermaking spread slowly (12) along the Silk Road. It reached the Islamic world by the 8th century, (13) where the technique was refined and paper mills were established. Muslims introduced paper to Europe during their occupation of Spain. By the 15th century, the invention of the printing press (14) had made books more accessible, and demand for paper increased dramatically.||Today, despite the digital revolution, paper remains essential (15) for many purposes. However, paper production has environmental costs, including deforestation and water pollution. This (16) has led to increased interest in recycling and developing sustainable alternatives. The challenge now is to balance our continued need for paper with environmental responsibility.

example.text: Paper is (0) a common material today that we rarely think about its history.

questions.explanation: Expresión 'credit goes to' indica a quién se atribuye el mérito.
Verbo 'wrote on' indica la superficie donde se escribía.
Artículo indefinido para describir 'a writing material'.
Preposición 'along' indica movimiento a lo largo de la Ruta de la Seda.
Pronombre relativo de lugar que se refiere al mundo islámico.
Pasado perfecto: 'had made' indica acción anterior a otra en el pasado.
Expresión 'essential for purposes' indica propósitos para los cuales es esencial.
Presente perfecto: 'has led to' indica resultado de los costos ambientales.
